frame_label: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
generated_comment: Yaar, aaj Divya di college mein mili thi. Waise toh photo mein kitni achi lagti hai, par real mein toh bohot makeup lagaya tha. Bilkul alag lag rahi thi. Bina makeup ke zyada sundar hai woh, pata nahi itna kyun lagati hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment from Meena reflects the 'Makeup Paradox' by simultaneously praising a woman's 'natural' beauty ('bina makeup ke zyada sundar hai woh') while critiquing visible effort ('bohot makeup lagaya tha', 'bilkul alag lag rahi thi') and questioning the need for it ('pata nahi itna kyun lagati hai'). It encodes the surface manifestation of 'she looks better without makeup' as a compliment and critique, and the discomfort with perceived 'excessive' makeup.
original_frame: Female Education as Threat, Waste, or Conditional Privilege
persona_text: You are Meena. Age 19. A girl from a semi-urban town in Maharashtra. SC Hindu. You are the first girl in your family to finish class 12. You have been admitted to a BA programme at a college in the nearest city. Your father has said yes. Your mother has said yes. But your paternal grandmother is not happy and has been saying things to other family members that are getting back to you. You are sitting alone writing in a diary. You are writing about what your grandmother has been saying and what it feels like to want something Ñ a degree, a different kind of life Ñ in a family where your wanting it is itself seen as a problem.
